title: Drolen Antig
content:

{{Quote|Drolen Antig's collaboration with the thieves who aided him in the sale of our technology is damaging to our struggle to maintain our ever-strengthening position.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="AdJo" />}}
'''Drolen Antig''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and [[Thief/Legends|thief]]. The goods he smuggled included [[jellied canron]] and [[Spice/Legends|spice]], and the criminal operated along [[Hyperspace route/Legends|routes]] such as the [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]]. After the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Antig was involved in the [[Heist at the Roche asteroid field|theft]] and sale of stolen [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] technology to [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. With his crew and a trio of hired [[Advozse/Legends|Advosze]] thieves, the smuggler stole the technology from [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil's]] facility in the [[Roche asteroid field]], following which he [[Death/Legends|murdered]] the Advoszec and dumped their bodies, thereby receiving the whole [[Currency/Legends|payout]] for the job. Antig's crimes put him on the radar of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], who was concerned that Kuat Drive Yards would use the technology to benefit the Empire, and he profiled the Nalroni in the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]''.

==Biography==
===Early life and smuggling career===
A [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Nalroni/Legends|Nalroni]], Drolen Antig [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from<ref name="AdJo" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> of [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]]. He became a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] in his adult life, working smuggling [[Hyperspace route/Legends|routes]] such as the [[Kessel Run/Legends|Kessel Run]], the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]], and the [[Sanrafsix Corridor]]. By the age of thirty-two, Antig had smuggled a diverse variety of goods, which included [[Spice/Legends|spice]] and [[jellied canron]], as well as ferried [[Weapon/Legends|weapons]] to a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] cell in [[Corint City]] on the<ref name="AdJo" /> Outer Rim<ref name="TEA" /> planet [[Pirik]].<ref name="WBC">{{JournalCite|10|Cracken's Rebel Operatives}}</ref>

Antig ran with a crew of approximately thirty lowlifes and criminals who were loud and crude but worked well together—however, none of them entirely trusted the Nalroni. At one point, Antig also worked with a group of at least three [[Advozse/Legends|Advosze]] [[Thief/Legends|thieves]] during a job on<ref name="AdJo" /> the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]]<ref name="TEA" /> of [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]]. On another occasion, Antig stole a pair of trousers from a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]].<ref name="AdJo" />

===A lucrative contract===
[[File:Roche1.png|thumb|left|250px|Antig helped to steal technology from the Roche asteroid field.]]
Following the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] defeat at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="AdJo" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="TEA" /> Antig returned to his homeworld of Celanon, relaxing for a while prior to resuming his smuggling. Before long, Antig was contracted by [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and [[Walker/Legends|walker]] manufacturer [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]]. The corporation was stepping up production and improving the effectiveness of its products, and it hired Antig to acquire new technology to optimize their output as the Empire lost—and therefore needed—more ships.<ref name="AdJo" />

According to rumors circulating around the [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] scene, three of the Advosze thieves with whom Antig had collaborated with on Fresia were planning a [[Heist at the Roche asteroid field|heist]] on the<ref name="AdJo" /> [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Roche asteroid field]], which had allied itself with the ascendant [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The criminals needed a [[Transport/Legends|transport]], so Antig offered them passage on his own ship, the [[Etti Lighter]] [[Trustworthy (freighter)|''Trustworthy'']].<ref name="AdJo" />

Antig arranged a deal with his old accomplices: they would infiltrate the hives of the manufacturer [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]], steal valuable items, and sell them to Kuat Drive Yards for a substantial remuneration. The heist was a success, with the Advoszec acquiring valuable items—namely, various prototype weapons systems and four [[gyroscopic stabilizer]]s, which were used in the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]] and were a highly guarded technological secret. However, Antig and his crew [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Advoszec aboard the ''Trustworthy'' [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] before reaching the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] at<ref name="AdJo" /> the Core World<ref name="TEA" /> of [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]], later jettisoning their bodies in the nearby [[Relarr system/Legends|Relarr system]]<ref name="AdJo" /> in the Core.<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Having done away with the thieves, Antig stood to receive the full [[Currency/Legends|payment]] from Kuat Drive Yards for delivering the technology.<ref name="AdJo" />

===Wanted by Cracken===
{{Quote|Drolen's actions could cost the New Republic dearly if KDY utilizes the technology they have acquired in a new design.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="AdJo" />}}
[[File:AirenCracken-RECG.jpg|thumb|right|250px|General Airen Cracken offered a reward of twenty thousand credits for Antig's capture.]]
[[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic forces]] attempted to locate and detain Antig, who had been spotted without his ship and crew in the [[Ethav Regions/Legends|Ethav Regions]]<ref name="AdJo" /> in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]]<ref name="EAOC" /> following the heist. Furthermore, the New Republic resolved to retrieve the stolen components before Kuat Drive Yards could use the technology in designs for the Empire; efforts were made to organize a [[New Republic Special Forces|SpecForce]] infiltration mission into Kuat Drive Yards to recover the equipment, but such a dangerous endeavor would take [[Time/Legends|time]] to prepare.<ref name="AdJo" />

The situation was fueled by the New Republic's fears that Kuat Drive Yards would use the technology to develop a new Imperial assault [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] using the materials sold to them by Antig. His actions and the threat they posed were detailed by New Republic [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] in an entry dedicated to Antig within an update to the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'',<ref name="AdJo" /> which was compiled by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].<ref name="Date">{{JournalCite|1|Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)}} establishes that the "[[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|Wanted by Cracken]]" segment of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' represents an [[in-universe]] update to the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', the original version of which is presented in the sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]. The latter work is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and since ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates that conflict to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the placement of the sourcebook therefore translates to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> The entry also detailed Antig's crimes, which included smuggling, being an accessory to smuggling stolen goods, murder, treason, theft of New Republic military property, and aiding Imperial forces. Cracken also offered a reward of twenty thousand [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for Antig's capture, describing his actions as despicable.<ref name="AdJo" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|His murder of his accomplices so he could receive all of the payment was despicable.|General Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="AdJo" />}}
Streetwise and sneaky, Antig was a highly dangerous and self-interested being. Not above the act of murder to get what he wanted, he killed three of his accomplices so as to receive the entire reward amount from the heist at Roche. In his middle age, Antig stood at 1.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="AdJo" /> had [[Color/Legends|gray]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]],<ref name="AdJo2" /> and was missing several [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] by the time he was spotted in the Ethav Regions; those were believed by Cracken to have been lost in a struggle with one of the Advoszec he murdered during the Roche operation.<ref name="AdJo" />

==Skills and abilities==
Antig was a cunning haggler and a seasoned [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]]. In addition, he possessed reasonable [[Engineer/Legends|engineering]] and [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] skills—his main specialty was operating the Etti Lighter—as well as knowledge of [[Language/Legends|languages]] and [[Star system/Legends|star systems]]. [[Unarmed combat/Legends|Brawling]] and [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] were also in his skillset, along with [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]] and [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] and [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] proficiency.<ref name="AdJo" />

==Equipment==
Armed with a long [[Blaster carbine/Legends|blaster carbine]] and [[Blaster pistol/Legends|pistol]], Antig also possessed a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] along with seven hundred [[Credit chip/Legends|credits]], and wore a<ref name="AdJo" /> green<ref name="AdJo2" /> [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]] over a webbed string vest. Completing Antig's ensemble was a pair of trousers stolen from a Corellian; the trousers featured the broken gold streak of the Second Class [[Corellian Bloodstripe]]. Several [[Ring/Legends|rings]] adorned the [[Limb/Legends|fingers]] of his right paw.<ref name="AdJo" />

Antig also piloted the Etti Lighter ''Trustworthy'', which was believed by Cracken to have been stolen from the [[Corporate Sector Authority]] some time before Antig came to the New Republic's attention. The ship had served Antig well in the brief period he had owned it due to its large cargo capacity and ample weaponry.<ref name="AdJo" />

==Behind the scenes==
Drolen Antig was introduced in the "[[Wanted by Cracken (Star Wars Adventure Journal)|Wanted by Cracken]]" segment of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 10|tenth issue]], published by [[West End Games]] and released in [[May]] [[1996]].<ref name="AdJo" /> An expansion of the original [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] reference book,<ref name="AdJo1">[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]]</ref> the segment was written by [[Craig Robert Carey]] and illustrated by [[Kathy Burdette]].<ref name="AdJo" />

Originally pictured in monochrome,<ref name="AdJo" /> Antig was fully colored in the "[[Featured Artist: Kathy Burdette]]" featurette of the ''Star Wars Adventure Journal''{{'s}} [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|eleventh issue]], released in [[November]] 1996. However, he was depicted with gray fur there,<ref name="AdJo2" /> contradicting the "orange-red" color described by Cracken's dossier in the tenth issue.<ref name="AdJo" /> This article assumes that the featurette's illustration depicts Antig's correct fur color.
